title: Responding to Official Letters from Ausländerbehörde
title_local: Antwort auf offizielle Schreiben vom Ausländeramt
summary: Official letters from the German immigration office (Ausländerbehörde) always have strict deadlines. Ignoring them can result in automatic rejection of your application or even loss of residence rights. The Widerspruch (appeal) deadline is typically 1 month.
steps: Read the letter carefully - note the deadline in the header (Frist)
Identify what is being requested (Antrag, Stellungnahme, or Entscheidung)
Gather required documents mentioned in the letter
Write a formal response in German if possible, or attach a translation
Submit before the deadline via email, mail, or in person
Keep a copy of everything you submit
If the deadline is missed, you may still have 1 month to file a Widerspruch
Contact a lawyer (Rechtsanwalt) if the letter threatens negative consequences
documents: Your passport or residence permit (Reisepass / Aufenthaltstitel)
Any requested documents listed in the letter
Previous correspondence related to your case
office: Ausländerbehörde (Foreigners' Registration Office) - your local one
fee: Free to respond; €50-100 for legal consultation if needed
notes: The German Widerspruch (appeal) must be filed within 1 month of receiving a negative decision. You can file it yourself or through a lawyer. If the Widerspruch is rejected, you can then file a Klage (lawsuit) at the Verwaltungsgericht within 1 month more.
